## 📝 Improvement Entries (Newest First)

### Template Entry (for reference):

```
---
Date: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS
Type: [A/B/C]
Title: [One line description]
Status: [SUGGESTED / APPROVED / APPLIED / REVERTED / FAILED]
Suggested By: [self-improvement agent]
Approved By: [user or automatic]

Description:
[Detailed explanation of improvement]

Files Modified:
- presentation-master.skill.md (1 change)
- presentation-master.agent.md (1 reference)

Risk Level: [Low / Medium / High]
Impact: [What gets better]

Implementation:
- Pattern: [What triggered this]
- Solution: [How it was fixed]
- Testing: [How it was validated]

Git Details:
- Commit: [commit-hash]
- Version: [v1.0.X → v1.0.Y]
- Branch: [main/dev]
- Tag: v1.0.Y

Outcome: ✅ SUCCESS / ⚠️ ISSUES / ❌ REVERTED
Notes: [Any relevant observations]

---
```

## 🎯 System Initialization Log

**Date**: 2026-04-07

### What Was Created

6. ✅ `.github/improvement-log.md` — This file
7. ✅ GitHub repo `presentation-master` — Backup & versioning

### Configuration

- Auto-suggest improvements: ON (after each presentation)
- Auto-approve Type A: OFF (wait for user confirmation)
- Git auto-commit: ON (when approved)
- Dev branch testing: Available

- Agent reliability: Not yet tested (pending first use)

## 📅 Future Improvements Will Appear Here

This section will auto-populate as you use the system:

- Each presentation generated
- Auto-analysis runs
- Suggestions appear
- You approve/reject
- Entries logged here
- Git commits tracked
- Version incremented

---

## 🔄 How Improvements Flow

```
PRESENTATION GENERATED
     ↓
self-improvement agent wakes up
     ↓
Analyzes for opportunities
     ↓
IF improvements found
  ├─ Type A: "Approve now?" → User response
  ├─ Type B: "Review first?" → Show diff → User response  
  ├─ Type C: "⚠️ High-risk. Dev test?" → User response
  └─ Type LOCKED: "Cannot improve locked rules."
     ↓
IF APPROVED
  ├─ Add entry to this log (SUGGESTED)
  ├─ Apply file changes locally
  ├─ Git commit & push
  ├─ Update entry (APPROVED → APPLIED)
  ├─ Version increment (1.0.X → 1.0.Y)
  └─ Alert: "v1.0.Y ready. Changes applied."
     ↓
IF dev-branch test chosen
  ├─ Test on temporary dev branch
  ├─ Run presentations with new rules
  ├─ If stable: merge to main + log (APPLIED)
  ├─ If fails: rollback + log (REVERTED)
  └─ Notify user of outcome
```

## 🔍 How to Read This Log

### Finding a Specific Improvement

```
Search for date: 2026-04-15
or title: "Add 4-author rule"
or type: Type B
or status: REVERTED
```

### Understanding Status

- **SUGGESTED**: Improvement found, shown to user, awaiting decision
- **APPROVED**: User said "yes", ready to apply
- **APPLIED**: Changes made locally, committed to Git
- **FAILED**: Applied but something broke
- **REVERTED**: Was broken, restored to previous version
- **TESTING**: On dev branch, under evaluation

### Git Integration

Each entry includes commit hash:
```
git show abc123def456  ← See exact changes
git revert abc123def456  ← Undo if needed
git checkout v1.0.5      ← Go back to that version
```

---

## 📞 Troubleshooting via Log

### "Why was system updated?"
→ Check this log for the date and reason

### "How do I undo it?"
→ Find the Git commit hash, then `git revert`

### "What changed between v1.0.3 and v1.0.4?"
→ Look for entries with those version tags

### "Has this improvement been made before?"
→ Search log by title/keyword
